question: Explain why acyl chlorides are more reactive than esters toward nucleophilic substitution.
prompt: The C-Cl bond is strongly ____ and chlorine is strongly electron withdrawing. The carbon atom of the carbonyl group is therefore more delta positive and is more readily attacked by nucleophiles.
full_answer: The C-Cl bond is strongly polar and chlorine is strongly electron withdrawing. The carbon atom of the carbonyl group is therefore more delta positive and is more readily attacked by nucleophiles.
